---
title: 'run-unbound'
---

Execute a built expression after replacing one of its nodes with input streamed
in over Arrow IPC. This is the CLI counterpart to streaming data into a served
unbound expression — useful for piping data between expressions without standing
up a Flight server.

## Usage

```bash
xorq run-unbound <build_path> [OPTIONS] < input.arrow
```

## Arguments

- **`build_path`** — Path to the build directory produced by `xorq build`.

## Options

| Option | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--to-unbind-hash` | inferred | Hash of the node to replace with the streamed input. |
| `--to-unbind-tag` | inferred | Tag of the node to replace (alternative to `--to-unbind-hash`). |
| `--typ` | none | Type of the node to unbind. |
| `-i`, `--instream` | stdin | File to read Arrow IPC record batches from. |
| `-o`, `--output-path` | stdout (arrow) / discard (other) | Path to write output. |
| `-f`, `--format` | `parquet` | Output format. One of `csv`, `json`, `parquet`, `arrow`. |
| `--limit` | unlimited | Maximum number of rows to output. |
| `--batch-size` | table default | Batch size for Arrow streaming output. |
| `--cache-dir` | `$XORQ_CACHE_DIR` or `~/.cache/xorq` | Directory for parquet cache files. |

## Examples

Stream input from a file:
```bash
xorq run-unbound builds/transform \
    --to-unbind-tag source_input \
    -i ./input.arrow \
    -o results.parquet
```

Pipe Arrow output from one expression directly into another:
```bash
xorq run builds/source -o - -f arrow \
  | xorq run-unbound builds/transform \
        --to-unbind-tag source_input \
        -o results.parquet
```

## See also

- [`serve-unbound`](serve-unbound.qmd) — serve the same unbound expression as a Flight endpoint
